title: Morning Hydration
body: Drink a glass of water as soon as you wake up to rehydrate your body after sleep and kickstart your metabolism.

title: Sleep Consistency
body: Go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on weekends, to regulate your circadian rhythm.

title: Daily Walking
body: Aim for at least 30 minutes of brisk walking daily to improve cardiovascular health and mood.

title: Morning Sunlight
body: Get 10-15 minutes of morning sunlight exposure to regulate your sleep-wake cycle and boost vitamin D.

title: Mindful Eating
body: Eat without distractions, chew slowly, and savor each bite to improve digestion and prevent overeating.

title: Digital Detox
body: Take a 30-minute break from screens before bed to improve sleep quality and reduce eye strain.

title: Posture Check
body: Sit up straight with shoulders back and feet flat on the floor to prevent back pain and improve breathing.

title: Protein First
body: Start your meals with protein-rich foods to stabilize blood sugar and keep you feeling full longer.

title: Breathing Break
body: Practice 4-7-8 breathing: inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8 to reduce stress instantly.

title: Hydration Reminder
body: Keep a water bottle visible and take sips throughout the day rather than drinking large amounts at once.

title: Sleep Environment
body: Keep your bedroom cool (65-68°F), dark, and quiet for optimal sleep quality.

title: Afternoon Stretch
body: Take a 5-minute stretching break in the afternoon to relieve muscle tension and improve circulation.

title: Intermittent Fasting
body: Try a 12-hour fasting window (e.g., 7 PM to 7 AM) to give your digestive system a rest.

title: Gratitude Practice
body: Write down three things you're grateful for each morning to start your day with positive focus.

title: Fiber Focus
body: Include high-fiber foods like vegetables, fruits, and whole grains in every meal for gut health.

title: Screen Distance
body: Keep screens at arm's length and adjust brightness to reduce eye strain and improve posture.

title: Evening Wind Down
body: Create a 30-minute pre-sleep routine with calming activities like reading or gentle stretching.

title: Healthy Fats
body: Include sources of healthy fats like avocados, nuts, and olive oil in your diet for brain health.

title: Movement Breaks
body: Stand up and move for 5 minutes every hour if you have a sedentary job to improve circulation.

title: Mindful Hydration
body: Drink water when you first feel thirsty - don't wait until you're very thirsty to hydrate.

title: Sleep Preparation
body: Avoid caffeine after 2 PM and heavy meals within 3 hours of bedtime for better sleep.

title: Post-Workout Recovery
body: Refuel with protein and carbohydrates within 30 minutes after exercise for optimal recovery.

title: Digital Sunset
body: Use night mode on devices after sunset to reduce blue light exposure that disrupts sleep.

title: Portion Awareness
body: Use smaller plates and bowls to naturally control portion sizes without feeling deprived.

title: Stress Release
body: Practice progressive muscle relaxation: tense and release each muscle group from toes to head.

title: Water Variety
body: Add lemon, cucumber, or mint to your water for flavor without added sugars or calories.

title: Sleep Position
body: Sleep on your back or side with proper pillow support to maintain spinal alignment.

title: Active Commuting
body: Walk or bike for short trips instead of driving to incorporate more movement into your day.

title: Meal Timing
body: Eat your largest meal earlier in the day when your metabolism is most active.

title: Breath Awareness
body: Take 10 deep breaths whenever you feel stressed to activate your parasympathetic nervous system.

title: Hydration Tracking
body: Aim for half your body weight in ounces of water daily (e.g., 150 lb person = 75 oz water).

title: Sleep Quality
body: Invest in a comfortable mattress and pillows - quality sleep is worth the investment.

title: Daily Movement
body: Incorporate strength training 2-3 times per week to maintain muscle mass and bone density.

title: Mindful Snacking
body: Choose snacks with protein and fiber like nuts or Greek yogurt to maintain energy levels.

title: Posture Reset
body: Set hourly reminders to check your posture and reset to a neutral spine position.
